paragraphs.text: Good initiative to combat monopolies on the internet. It is unfair how companies and online platforms censor content with a false pretense of "protecting children" by breaching the rights of individuals and privacies of individuals making them feel like bad people for not allowing being watched 24/7 casting this air of accusation of mischief or serious malicious degeneracy on regular people. We have seen time and time again personal data be breached by companies swearing up and down that they will protect our personal data and in the end it still gets stolen (including government websites) and I would not like for that to happen with my data. I also think it should be addressed the constant grooming of children by pushing gambling practices dressed up as "cute anime games" onto them. There is a way to stop one thing without infringing on grown adults' rights to privacy. I think there should be added pressure to parents or guardians responsible for children under the age of 18 and resources to help them navigate the internet for children. There is not a one option fixes all button here, there needs to be complexity to fight for the internet to be secure AND free from manipulation that requires nuances and experts on the subject.
